violations.text: Observed the following equipment in poor repair/need of adjustment: -cold draw #2 gasket -lowboy door gaskets -refrig. door gasket -freezer door gasket Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. Per PIC gaskets are on order. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment shall sanitize in 3-bay sink until dishwasher is serviced and in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

(Mac & cheese) was observed held at (127) degrees Fahrenheit. Per Staff mac & cheese was placed in hot hold less than an hour before inspection. Staff reheated mac & cheese to (147) degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Proper hot hold reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

(Diced tomatoes and sliced tomatoes) was observed held at (47-48) degrees Fahrenheit. Tomato was placed in unit 2 hours before inspection. PIC moved tomatoes to walk-in cooler to rapidly cool at time of inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Proper cold-holding reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Several foods located in walk-in coolers were observed passed their discarded date. PIC voluntarily disposed of foods at time of inspection. See disposal form for list of foods. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Proper date marking reviewed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

Observed the following equipment in poor repair/need of adjustment: -cold draw #2 gaskets -lowboy door gaskets -refrig. door gasket -freezer door gasket and latch -dishwasher sink's faucet connection (leaking) Equipment shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/25/2026

Observed wall tiles in the dry storage area and dishwashing area is in poor repair (missing/need to be replaced); floors throughout facility need regrouged, water is pooling between tiles in some areas of the kitchen. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2026

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2026

The front door to the outside and back door to the outside were observed not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. -Per interview of staff, a service order has been placed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Some cracks and crevices observed in the kitchen area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. -Per interview of staff, a service order has been placed to seal all gaps/holes. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025
The menu is lacking an adequate allergen warning for consumers. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

Inspector observed biofilm dripping onto ice during inspection. Due to this contamination the ice was considered to be adulterated and PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Ice machine taken out of service to be sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) fish was observed thawing in walk-in cooler without sufficiently opening the package to allow for adequate oxygen transfer. Inspector determined that the fish was below 37 degrees Fahrenheit and did not need to be disposed. Packages were opened at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/17/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
The floor throughout all areas of the kitchen is in poor repair (grout missing). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
Multiple dead flies were observed by the door in the back corner of the dining area. The establishment must remove dead pests from establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting (bottom left corner). Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

The floor grout in the kitchen area was in poor repair. Pooling water and old food debris was observed in the crevices. Floor grout/tiles to be repaired/replaced to allow for the floor to be easily cleanable. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
